capture.cameraPose: IWER Meta Quest 3 default pose, controller dev UI visible

notes: The screenshot proves a rendered ED chest pain station shell and visible clinical text, but the actual patient, staff, family, and equipment are not sufficiently visible from this pose to judge clinical fidelity.
The visible scene is dominated by flat panels, primitive controller markers, and simplified geometry; it is useful for layout iteration but not actor, equipment, clothing, skin, or rigging realism.
Some in-scene text is readable, including 'Input Evidence' and 'Session: In Full VR', but several panels are clipped, angled, or partially hidden by the IWER controller overlays.
IWER controller widgets and transform handles are visible, but this screenshot does not prove physical controller latency, hand tracking, hand-only locomotion, or user comfort.
The controller panels occlude lower scene content and the close camera pose makes scale hard to assess; a physical headset run and cleaner capture pose remain necessary.
This artifact supports only an emulated IWER auto-entry and adversarial visual-review claim; it does not replace physical Quest foreground capture or production XR readiness evidence.
